title: rosa arkansana f. setulosa
intro: Rosa arkansana f. setulosa, also known as Arkansas rose or prairie rose, is a subspecies of wild rose native to North America, characterized by its fragrant pink flowers and thorny stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Rosa arkansana f. setulosa include soothing skin irritations, reducing inflammation, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, tinctures, and salves, and are often used topically to calm skin conditions and promote wound healing.
